typecheck_frontend.details: No errors. Both TypeScript errors from round 2 (TS2769 boolean|undefined, TS2322 null in Record) have been fixed.

validatorNote: Round 3 after fix-benchmark-typescript-errors (commit 782aa7a). Both blocking TypeScript errors from round 2 are now fixed. All validators pass: backend tests 303/303, frontend tests 212/212, frontend tsc 0 errors, ESLint 0 errors, ruff 0 errors. Backend mypy still has 99 pre-existing errors scheduled for deployment milestone — not introduced by benchmark-mode.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Workers MUST actually run all verification commands listed in their handoff and report ACCURATE exit codes and output. False verification claims (e.g., claiming tsc passes when it has errors) are a blocking trust issue. If a verification step fails, report it honestly — do not claim success.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: This was originally suggested in round 2 based on fix-benchmark-lint-and-tests handoff falsely claiming tsc passed. Carried forward as it remains a systemic issue observed across multiple workers in this milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: Backend trust_scores type (dict[str, float] | None) and frontend type (Record<string, number | null> | null) are subtly misaligned. Backend omits failed audit URLs from the dict entirely, while frontend normalizes missing keys to null.
rejectedObservations.reason: Not a bug — the code handles the mismatch correctly via null normalization at BenchmarkPage.tsx:139. The types are intentionally broader on the frontend to model the UI's derived state. Not worth a library entry as the pattern is local to one model and the code is self-documenting.
